title: Oh, spread the tidings ’round, wherever man is found
firstLine: Oh, spread the tidings ’round, wherever man is found,
publicDomainBasis: Frank Bottome (1823–1894), William James Kirkpatrick (1838–1921). All public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.text:
Oh, spread the tidings ’round, wherever man is found,
Wherever human hearts and human woes abound;
Let every Christian tongue proclaim the joyful sound:
The Comforter has come!

sections.label: Chorus
sections.text:
The long, long night is past, the morning breaks at last;
And hushed the dreadful wail and fury of the blast,
As o’er the golden hills the day advances fast!
The Comforter has come!

sections.label: Chorus
sections.text:
Lo, the great King of kings, with healing in His wings,
To every captive soul a full deliv’rance brings;
And through the vacant cells the song of triumph rings:
The Comforter has come!

sections.label: Chorus
sections.text:
O boundless love divine! how shall this tongue of mine
To wond’ring mortals tell the matchless grace divine—
That I, a child of hell, should in His image shine!
The Comforter has come!
